summary: Tested Admin Impersonation feature (BACKEND-A..H + FRONTEND-A..F) for PropManage. Backend: 17/17 pytest cases pass. Frontend: all impersonation flows work end-to-end — admin starts impersonation from /admin Users table, gets cookie-swapped to target's session, sees red sticky banner with 2h countdown (119:57) + target name, can stop, returns to /admin. COMPLIANCE → 'Impersonări' audit log table loads 20+ rows. Client Settings → Privacy → 'Cine a accesat contul tău' shows 16 access-history items without PII (IP/UA stripped).

minor.issue: Spec says nested impersonation → 409, but route returns 403 'Insufficient permissions' instead. RCA: `require_role('admin')` runs BEFORE the `if admin.get('impersonation'): raise 409` check. While impersonating, current user's role == target's role (e.g. 'client'), so require_role fails first → 403. The 409 path is unreachable dead code. Practical behavior (nesting blocked) is preserved, but status code deviates from spec.

When body misses required fields (e.g. password/confirmation/code), Pydantic returns 422 BEFORE block_in_impersonation runs. So an impersonator sending a malformed body sees 422 instead of 403. Not a security hole (no action performed), but inconsistent. Block check should move into a Depends() that runs before request body parsing, or also be a router-level guard.

action_items: (Optional/Polish) Move the active-impersonation 409 check ahead of `require_role('admin')` so spec-compliant 409 is returned for nested impersonation attempts. Could be done by treating impersonating admins as admin-role in require_role, or by adding an early check inside get_current_user before role gating.
(Optional) Use block_in_impersonation as a FastAPI dependency (Depends) on dangerous routes so it triggers regardless of body validation order, returning 403 deterministically.
critical_code_review_comments: impersonation.py: Cookie-swap relies on Set-Cookie of access_token; on stop, admin_access_token is correctly cleared. JWT TTL is exactly 7200s as specified. Audit logging captures admin/target/IP/UA. Access-history endpoint correctly strips IP+UA for data-subject view.

deps.py block_in_impersonation: Currently called inline; consider promoting to a reusable Depends() guard for consistency across all sensitive endpoints (change-password OK, but 2fa-disable + account-delete only check after body validation).
Frontend ImpersonationBanner shows full target name + role + admin email + GDPR session ID — good for compliance UX. Countdown updates correctly (saw 119:57 right after start, expected ~2h).
AdminImpersonationLogs displays admin, target, reason, duration, IP columns; search input works client-side.
test_credentials: Used: admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!, client@propmanage.io/Client123!, specialist@propmanage.io/Spec123! — all working.
seed_data_creation: Tests created ~10 audit log rows during the run; these are real impersonation sessions of client@propmanage.io with reason prefixes like 'QA ...' / 'Stop cycle test ...'. Safe to keep — they exercise the access-history empty→populated transition for the test client.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Impersonation backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_phase_imp_impersonation.py and rely on the admin/users endpoint (paginated with limit/skip). The client user-id for client@propmanage.io is 6a11d70d600be19667009c8e and lives around skip=50. Frontend impersonation banner uses data-testid='impersonation-banner' (target/timer/stop subtestids). AdminImpersonationLogs table uses data-testid='impersonation-logs'. Client privacy modal section uses data-testid='access-history-section' with -list / -empty children.
rca of the issue: Two minor status-code mismatches (see backend_issues.minor). Both stem from FastAPI dependency-resolution order — role check and body validation both fire before the impersonation guard in their respective endpoints. Functionally safe (nested impersonation and dangerous endpoint mutations are still blocked), but the returned HTTP status differs from the PRD spec (409 → 403; 403 → 422 on malformed bodies).
